meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/intune) — portal paths, setting labels, defaults, and Data Protection Framework baseline values grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: App Protection Policies (MAM) protect org data INSIDE managed Microsoft apps (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, Edge, Word/Excel/PowerPoint) and don't require the device to be enrolled — but they only apply when users use those managed apps. A consequence: company mail must be accessed via Outlook, not a generic iOS/Android mail client.
Requires Intune Plan 1 (included in Microsoft 365 Business Premium).
Create ONE policy per platform — a separate iOS/iPadOS policy and Android policy — under Apps > App protection policies > Create policy.

The values below follow Microsoft's Data Protection Framework baseline (enterprise level). Confirm each against the client's tier; the Inforcer master doesn't pin exact PIN length / grace values, so treat them as the recommended starting point and record any client-specific choice.
Roll out with user comms: the first time a user opens a managed app they'll be prompted to set an app PIN, and copy/paste out to personal apps will be limited.

requiredSettings.label: Platform
requiredSettings.value: iOS/iPadOS · Apps = the Microsoft 365 apps you want protected (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, Edge, Office apps)

requiredSettings.label: Data protection — Send org data to other apps
requiredSettings.value: Policy managed apps (keeps org data within Intune-managed apps)

requiredSettings.label: Data protection — Save copies of org data
requiredSettings.value: Block (allow only OneDrive for Business, SharePoint, Photo Library)

requiredSettings.label: Data protection — Restrict cut, copy, and paste between apps
requiredSettings.value: Policy managed apps with paste in (blocks copy of org data out to personal apps)

requiredSettings.label: Data protection — Screen capture
requiredSettings.value: Block

requiredSettings.label: Data protection — Encrypt org data
requiredSettings.value: Require

requiredSettings.label: Access requirements — PIN for access
requiredSettings.value: Require (numeric; min length per baseline, commonly 6)

requiredSettings.label: Access requirements — Recheck access requirements after
requiredSettings.value: 30 minutes

requiredSettings.label: Conditional launch
requiredSettings.value: Offline grace period → Wipe data (e.g. 90 days); Jailbroken/rooted devices → Block access; Min OS version → Block access

steps: In the Intune admin center → Apps → App protection policies → Create policy → iOS/iPadOS.
Basics: name it (e.g. 'iOS - MAM - App Protection'). Apps: target Public apps / the Microsoft 365 apps to protect.

Data protection tab: set Send org data to other apps = Policy managed apps; Save copies of org data = Block (allow OneDrive/SharePoint/Photo Library); Restrict cut, copy, and paste = Policy managed apps with paste in; Screen capture = Block; Encrypt org data = Require.
Access requirements tab: PIN for access = Require; set PIN type/length per baseline; Recheck access requirements after = 30 minutes.
Conditional launch tab: set the offline grace period (wipe), block jailbroken/rooted devices, and a minimum OS version.
Assignments: target the user group(s). Review + create.
Note: users will be prompted for an app PIN on first launch of a managed app and can't paste org data into personal apps — brief them first.

requiredSettings.label: Platform
requiredSettings.value: Android · Apps = the Microsoft 365 apps you want protected (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, Edge, Office apps)

requiredSettings.label: Data protection — Send org data to other apps
requiredSettings.value: Policy managed apps (keeps org data within Intune-managed apps)

requiredSettings.label: Data protection — Save copies of org data
requiredSettings.value: Block (allow only OneDrive for Business, SharePoint, Photo Library)

requiredSettings.label: Data protection — Restrict cut, copy, and paste between apps
requiredSettings.value: Policy managed apps with paste in (blocks copy of org data out to personal apps)

requiredSettings.label: Data protection — Screen capture and Google Assistant
requiredSettings.value: Block

requiredSettings.label: Data protection — Encrypt org data
requiredSettings.value: Require

requiredSettings.label: Access requirements — PIN for access
requiredSettings.value: Require (numeric; min length per baseline, commonly 6)

requiredSettings.label: Access requirements — Recheck access requirements after
requiredSettings.value: 30 minutes

requiredSettings.label: Conditional launch
requiredSettings.value: Offline grace period → Wipe data (e.g. 90 days); Rooted devices → Block access; Min OS version → Block access; SafetyNet/Play Integrity device attestation as required

steps: In the Intune admin center → Apps → App protection policies → Create policy → Android.
Basics: name it (e.g. 'Android - MAM - App Protection'). Apps: target Public apps / the Microsoft 365 apps to protect.

Data protection tab: Send org data to other apps = Policy managed apps; Save copies of org data = Block (allow OneDrive/SharePoint/Photo Library); Restrict cut, copy, and paste = Policy managed apps with paste in; Screen capture and Google Assistant = Block; Encrypt org data = Require.
Access requirements tab: PIN for access = Require; set PIN type/length per baseline; Recheck access requirements after = 30 minutes.
Conditional launch tab: set the offline grace period (wipe), block rooted devices, a minimum OS version, and Play Integrity/SafetyNet attestation if used.
Assignments: target the user group(s). Review + create.
Note: company mail must be used through Outlook (not a generic Android mail client), and users get an app PIN + copy/paste limits — communicate ahead of rollout.

requiredSettings.label: Mark devices with no compliance policy assigned as
requiredSettings.value: Not compliant (default is Compliant — set to Not compliant so only confirmed-compliant devices pass Conditional Access)

requiredSettings.label: Compliance status validity period (days)
requiredSettings.value: 30 (default; configurable 1–120). A device that doesn't report in this window is treated as noncompliant.

requiredSettings.label: Where the per-device grace period lives
requiredSettings.value: NOT here — it's the 'Mark device noncompliant' action inside each compliance policy (default 0 days = immediate). Set a grace period there if you want a remediation window.

steps: In the Intune admin center → Devices → Compliance → Compliance policy settings (also reachable via Endpoint security → Device compliance → Compliance policy settings).
Set 'Mark devices with no compliance policy assigned as' = Not compliant.
Confirm 'Compliance status validity period (days)' = 30 (or the client's chosen value, 1–120).

Note: switching the no-policy default to Not compliant only bites devices that have NO compliance policy — make sure your compliance policies are assigned first, or unmanaged/newly-enrolled devices can lose access. Per-device remediation windows are set on each compliance policy's Mark device noncompliant action, not here.
